That’s a chilling direction to take the story, and it really drives home the idea of how dependence on these systems could make opting out nearly impossible. That’s a haunting yet compelling twist—it really underscores how pervasive the system’s reach is. The idea that even an underground group isn’t safe because of the system’s ability to track everything adds a whole new layer of paranoia and hopelessness. You could show how the protagonist initially feels relief at finding others off the grid, only to have that hope crushed when they explain the extent of the system’s surveillance.
